codeReview.summary: The assessment list page is well-implemented following project conventions. It creates the /assessments route with proper authentication checking, displays all required columns (Peserta, Template, Skor, Status), includes color-coded status badges with Indonesian labels, and provides a link to start new assessments. The code uses TypeScript properly with interfaces for all data structures and follows the TanStack Start pattern with createServerFn.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed AGENTS.md conventions: all UI labels are in Indonesian (Peserta, Template, Skor, Status, Belum Dimulai, etc.), used consistent naming patterns matching other list pages (partners.tsx, interviews.tsx), and implemented proper pagination.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/routes/assessments.tsx lines 114-117 contain Indonesian column headers; lines 36-48 contain Indonesian status labels

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the fullstack-worker procedure correctly: read mission context, implemented server function with proper validation, created route component with loader, ran typecheck and tests before handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows commandsRun: npm run typecheck (exitCode 0), npm run test (exitCode 0), curl for route verification

summary: Assessment list page implementation is complete and passes review. The /assessments route displays assessment attempts with all required columns: Peserta (with name and email), Template (with title and code), Skor (with total, objective, and essay breakdown), and Status (with color-coded badges). The '+ Penilaian Baru' button links to start new assessments. Code quality is high with proper TypeScript interfaces, server function with input validation, pagination, loading states, and responsive design. Follows existing patterns from partner-list-page and interview-list-page.
